THE ISSUE OF WHETHER THE CLAIMS ARE SUBJECT TO AN ENFORCEABLE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE MAY NOT BE RAISED BY DEMURRER; RATHER, THE ISSUE MUST BE RAISED BY A MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BANK SAL OPPENHEIM JR & CIE (SCHWEIZ) AG'S MOTION TO VACATE ARBITRATION ORDER. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. THE COURT CONSIDERS CONDUCT THAT OCCURRED AFTER JUDGE ALVARADO'S ORDER AND THE CURRENT MOTION IS NOT A MOTION TO RECONSIDER THAT ORDER. AAA RULES ADEQUATELY ADDRESS COSTS. DEFENDANTS' REFUSAL TO SHARE COSTS DOES NOT CONSTITUTE A WAIVER OF THEIR RIGHT TO ARBITRATE AS SUCH REFUSAL IS NOT CONTRARY TO ANY OF THE COSTS PROVISIONS PROVIDED IN THE AGREEMENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion To Vacate Arbitration
Off Calendar May-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF BANK SAL OPPENHEIM JR & CIE (SCHWEIZ) AG'S MOTION TO VACATE ARBITRATION ORDER

Appearances:
Matthew F. Quint of Wilson & Quint, LLP (288.6700) appearing for defendant and responding party
David S. Schwartz of the Law Offices of David H. Schwartz (975.2838) appearing for plaintiff and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. The court considers conduct that occurred after Judge Alvarado's order and the current motion is not a motion to reconsider that order. AAA rules adequately address costs. Defendants' refusal to share costs does not constitute a waiver of their right to arbitrate as such refusal is not contrary to any of the costs provisions provided in the agreement. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PLANTAGENET CAPITAL FUND, L.P., PLANTAGENET CAPITAL FUND II, L.P., PLANTAGENET CAPITAL PARTNERS, L.P., PLANTAGENET CAPITAL MANAGEMENT L.L.C. and C. DEREK ANDERSON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - OVERRULED. 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. THE ISSUE OF WHETHER THE CLAIMS ARE SUBJECT TO AN ENFORCEABLE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE MAY NOT BE RAISED BY DEMURRER; RATHER, THE ISSUE MUST BE RAISED BY A MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.
